# 2026-07-01: direct JSON StringExpected root cause locked and fixed
- 根因锁定：4444 非流 `/v1/responses` 单轮 direct 请求在 provider 200 后本地抛 `StringExpected`；live red `openai-responses-router-gpt-5.5-20260701T063346584-436298-9608` 证明无 continuation/history 也复现，非旧上下文问题。
- 唯一错误层：`sendPipelineResponse -> prepareResponsesJsonClientDispatchPlanForHttp` 对 `continuationOwner=direct` 的 JSON response 仍进入 Responses client projection；direct 路径没有 projection 所需 `requestContext.context.toolsRaw`，违反 direct passthrough + hooks 合同。
- 修复：`prepareResponsesJsonClientDispatchPlanForHttp` 对 `continuationOwner === 'direct'` 直接返回原 body，不进入 Responses projection；handler JSON dispatch 显式传入 `result.continuationOwner`。
- 验证：bridge 定向 Jest 5 passed；root tsc PASS；`verify:responses-handler-single-bridge-surface` PASS；`verify:responses-direct-tool-shape-contract` PASS；`build:base` PASS；全局安装 0.90.3382；4444 health OK；live red 非流请求由 HTTP 502/StringExpected 变 HTTP 200；spark streaming shape 样本路由到 `ykk.gpt-5.3-codex-spark` 且 HTTP 200。
2026-07-01: 4444 ykk latest audit: request openai-responses-router-gpt-5.5-20260701T064817024-436348-9658 hit ykk.gpt-5.4-mini first and got provider.send HTTP_502, then rerouted through asxs/XL and finally failed on 1token with UPSTREAM_STREAM_TIMEOUT. Same 4444 thinking ykk minimal JSON and SSE probes returned HTTP 200, so ykk is not globally unavailable; root body for the original 502 was not captured because router-direct snapshot entryPort was read back from MetadataCenter and became undefined. Fixed direct snapshot entryPort to use portConfig.port; installed/restarted 4444; no new entryPort missing after restart.

- Rust/NAPI `build_servertool_cli_projection_runtime_branch_json()` now receives `projectedToolCallId + base`, collects additional client tool calls in Rust, and constructs the client-visible projection branch.

# 2026-07-01: Responses SSE encode validation-disable bypass removed
- Red evidence: `tests/sharedmodule/responses-sse-usage-no-fallback.spec.ts` first failed because `sequenceResponse(..., { enableValidation:false })` silently skipped an unknown `output` item and still emitted `response.completed` / `response.done`.
- Fix: `responses-sequencer.ts` no longer exposes or checks `enableValidation`; response shape validation, output-item type validation, and content-part max checks always fail-fast. `responses-json-to-sse-converter.ts` no longer passes conversion config into a validation-disable switch.

- Red evidence: `responses-json-to-sse-context-no-dead-state.spec.ts` first failed because `responses-json-to-sse-converter.ts` still contained `CONTEXT_TTL_MS`, `MAX_CONTEXTS`, `pruneResponsesContexts`, converter-level `contexts` map, and `getContext/getActiveContexts/clearContext`.

# 2026-07-01: Responses JSON->SSE converter config defaults removed
- Red evidence: `responses-json-to-sse-context-no-dead-state.spec.ts` first failed because `responses-json-to-sse-converter.ts` still imported `DEFAULT_RESPONSES_CONVERSION_CONFIG`, exposed constructor config, returned `getConfig`, and used `context.options.chunkSize || this.config.defaultChunkSize` / `delayMs || defaultDelayMs`.
- Fix: converter no longer owns config defaults or `getConfig`; it only forwards explicit `chunkSize` / `delayMs` when present. Missing chunk config falls through to sequencer/native defaults instead of a converter-level second truth.

No internal body metadata leak was observed in the captured shape.
- Rebuilt/installed/restarted 4444 to 0.90.3384 after adding `estimatedTextChars` for future direct failure snapshots; `/health` returned version 0.90.3384.

# 2026-07-01: stopless two-sided blackbox evidence audit
- User correction: previous stopless closure answer was too high-level; required evidence is client request/response and provider request/response blackbox observation, not only unit/blackbox PASS.
- Evidence run: temporary instrumented copy of `scripts/tests/stopless-contract-blackbox.mjs` printed `[stopless-evidence]` for `no_schema`, `invalid_schema`, and `next_step`; command exited `STATUS=0` and temp file was removed.
- Observed contract for all three cases: client first response has `exec_command` containing `routecodex hook run reasoningStop`, does not leak raw `"reasoningStop"`, and exposes required action/tool-call surface; provider first request contains `<rcc_stop_schema>`, conditional-required guidance, `reasoningStop` tool, and `reasoningStop.required=["stopreason"]`.
- Observed invalid-schema feedback: `invalid_schema` produced `schemaFeedbackReasonCode="stop_schema_next_step_missing"` and `missingFields=["next_step"]`; provider second request includes expected feedback text `stop_schema_next_step_missing`.
- Observed CoI/internal-tool conversion boundary: second provider request restores CLI result as internal `reasoningStop` `function_call` + matching `function_call_output`, with `restoredOutputTriggerHint` matching the case and no raw `stop_message_auto` CLI payload leak.

# 2026-07-01: Responses SSE decode optional IO options fixed
- Red evidence: `responses-sse-decode-no-ts-wire-synthesis` first failed because `ResponsesSseToJsonConverter.convertSseToJson(bodyText)` without debug options crashed on `options.requestId`; the same issue blocked real 4444 provider-response replay.
- Fix: `convertSseToJson` now treats options as optional IO/debug context, uses deterministic internal request id `responses-sse-decode` when omitted, and still delegates final response truth to Rust `parseRespFormatEnvelopeWithNative`.
- Verification: focused Responses SSE Jest PASS 5/5; `verify:sse-architecture-boundary`, `verify:responses-handler-single-bridge-surface`, `verify:responses-sse-business-module`, sharedmodule/root tsc, `build:base`, and `git diff --check` PASS.
- Replay evidence: real sample `/Volumes/extension/.rcc/codex-samples/openai-responses/ports/4444/req_1782794773576_s7okhowx0/provider-response_1.json` replayed via dist converter with `pathPort=4444`, `status=completed`, `outputCount=2`, `hasId=true`; sample JSON itself has no embedded entryPort.

# 2026-07-01: 4444 direct 502 request-shape investigation
- Current evidence: 4444 failed direct Responses samples (0.90.3387/3388) have providerRequestShape input object=2. These are not metadata leaks; shape roleCounts shows they are two role-bearing message-like input entries missing type.
- Direct path owner evidence: ResponsesProvider.processIncomingDirect uses buildPassthroughResponsesBody(request) and router-direct only applies model/thinking/capability hooks; it bypasses Rust Hub req_inbound normalization.
- Success comparison: 4444 earlier gpt-5.4-mini success and 5520 gpt-5.4-mini success have no object input items. 5520 success has larger JSON chars (~400K) than 4444 failed estimated text (~317K), so size alone is not proven root cause.
- Candidate unique fix: reuse Rust Responses input normalization/provider-wire contract at direct provider wire boundary, not TS ad-hoc sanitize and not MetadataCenter. Need red test proving untyped role/content message cannot reach direct provider send.

- 2026-07-01 chat SSE fail-fast slice
  - Current state: `sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/src/sse/json-to-sse/sequencers/chat-sequencer.ts` still wraps `sequenceChatResponse()` in a catch and yields `buildErrorEvent(...)`, so malformed chat JSON→SSE input becomes a synthetic SSE error frame instead of a hard failure.
  - Tests updated to expect stream rejection for invalid usage, finish_reason, legacy function_call args, missing function_call id, missing tool_call args, and missing delta role.
  - Required follow-up: add architecture gate markers for the chat sequencer catch/buildErrorEvent path, remove the catch/import, then run focused jest + verify:sse-architecture-boundary + build.

- 2026-07-01 Responses content_part.done partless TS synthesis removal
  - Red evidence: focused `responses-sse-content-part-descriptor-native` first failed because exported TS `buildContentPartDoneEvent()` accepted `undefined` content and generated a partless `response.content_part.done` instead of failing.
  - Fix: `buildContentPartDoneEvent()` now requires a concrete `ResponsesContent`, the native TS wrapper no longer accepts optional `contentPart`, and `verify:sse-architecture-boundary` forbids the old optional/ternary markers.
  - Verification: focused Jest PASS 5/5; `verify:sse-architecture-boundary`, `verify:responses-sse-business-module`, `verify:hub-response-provider-sse-materialization`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, sharedmodule/root `tsc`, `verify:servertool-rust-only`, servertool execution/audit Jest, `build:base BUILD_EXIT:0`, `git diff --check`, real 4444 Responses SSE decode->encode replay, and source output_text content_part.done replay PASS.

- 2026-07-01 build block cleared after servertool wiki sync
  - Block: `npm run build:base` first failed at `verify:architecture-wiki-sync` because `docs/architecture/wiki/servertool-ownership-map.md` was out of sync, then at `verify:architecture-wiki-html-sync` because the matching HTML artifact was stale.
  - Fix: regenerated wiki markdown with `node scripts/architecture/render-architecture-wiki-pages.mjs` and HTML with `npm run render:architecture-wiki-html`; focused servertool tests verified the current response-stage/auto-hook boundaries.
  - Verification: focused servertool Jest 53/53 PASS; final `npm run build:base` exit `0`, including architecture gates, function-map gates, servertool Rust-only gate, llmswitch-core build, root TypeScript, webui build, asset copy, and CLI permission fix. Build version advanced to `0.90.3424`. No server restart was performed.

- 2026-07-01 Chat JSON->SSE context cache removed

# 2026-07-01: servertool execution loop effect carrier moved off TS Error message extraction
- Red evidence: `execution-queue-shell.ts` still did `lastErr instanceof Error ? lastErr.message : lastErr`, so TS was deciding handler error truth before native effect planning.
- Fix: queue shell now passes raw `lastErr` to `planServertoolExecutionLoopEffectWithNative()`. Native bridge encodes `Error` as `{ message }` for JSON transport, and Rust `execution_loop_effect_contract.rs` owns string/number/bool/object.message normalization and `unknown` fallback.
- Verification: focused servertool Jest PASS 4/4; `npx tsc -p sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/tsconfig.json --pretty false` PASS; `npm run verify:servertool-rust-only` PASS.

# 2026-07-01: Anthropic/Gemini JSON->SSE converter context cache removed
- Boundary correction: SSE layer must stay wire/frame/codec-only and must not own business semantics, lifecycle decisions, recovery, or compatibility synthesis.
- Red evidence: `anthropic-gemini-json-to-sse-context-no-dead-state` and `verify:sse-architecture-boundary` first failed on Anthropic/Gemini converter-level context cache markers: `private contexts = new Map`, `this.contexts.set(`, and `this.contexts.delete(`.
- Fix: `AnthropicJsonToSseConverter` and `GeminiJsonToSseConverter` now keep only per-call local conversion context; dead converter-level context maps were physically removed.
- Required follow-up verification: focused Anthropic/Gemini Jest, SSE architecture boundary, sharedmodule/root TypeScript, response SSE business gate, function-map gate, real 4444 replay, build, and exact-file commit.

# 2026-07-01: Chat SSE converter config surface removed
- Boundary correction: Chat SSE converter surfaces are IO/codec shells; they must not own converter-level default config or expose config introspection as a second semantic truth.
- Red evidence: `chat-json-to-sse-context-no-dead-state` and `verify:sse-architecture-boundary` first failed on `DEFAULT_CHAT_CONVERSION_CONFIG`, converter `private config`, constructor config injection, `this.config`, `getConfig`, and public `ChatConversionConfig`.
- Fix: `ChatJsonToSseConverterRefactored` and `ChatSseToJsonConverter` now pass only explicit per-call options into sequencer/dispatcher; `ChatConversionConfig`, `DEFAULT_CHAT_CONVERSION_CONFIG`, and `ChatSseEventStream.getConfig()` were physically removed.
- Verification so far: focused Chat Jest PASS 36/36; `verify:sse-architecture-boundary`, `verify:responses-sse-business-module`, sharedmodule/root `tsc`, and `verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS. Source-import replay needs dist build first.

# 2026-07-01: Anthropic/Gemini SSE converter config surface removed
- Boundary correction: Anthropic/Gemini JSON->SSE converters are per-call IO/codec shells and must not hold converter-level default config or constructor config injection as a second semantic/config truth.
- Red evidence: `anthropic-gemini-json-to-sse-context-no-dead-state` and `verify:sse-architecture-boundary` first failed on converter `private config`, constructor config injection, `this.config.*`, and `DEFAULT_ANTHROPIC_CONVERSION_CONFIG` / `DEFAULT_GEMINI_CONVERSION_CONFIG`.
- Fix: `AnthropicJsonToSseConverter` and `GeminiJsonToSseConverter` now pass only explicit per-call options into their sequencers; converter-level default config storage and constructor config injection were physically removed.

# 2026-07-01: servertool registry builtin canonicalName contract moved to native wrapper
- Slice: `registry-orchestration-shell.ts` no longer throws a local `return_builtin`/`canonicalName` contract error. `planServertoolRegistryLookupFromSkeletonWithNative()` now trims and validates `canonicalName`, and rejects `action=return_builtin` without a non-empty canonical name before the TS shell sees the plan.
- Gate: `verify-servertool-rust-only` now forbids `native registry lookup returned builtin without canonicalName` and `if (!actionPlan.canonicalName)` in `registry-orchestration-shell.ts`, and requires the thin-shell call `name: actionPlan.canonicalName as string`.

- Commit scope note: unrelated dirty package/router-direct/SSE/VR/native-exports/build-info files remain excluded from this servertool slice.

# 2026-07-01: servertool registry auto-hook sourceIndex rematch removed from TS
- Slice: `registry-orchestration-shell.ts` no longer rehydrates native auto-hook descriptors by `descriptor.sourceIndex` inside TS. `planServertoolRegistryBuiltinAutoHookEntriesWithNative()` now consumes builtin hook metadata, rebinds the native descriptor plan to registration/execution at the native wrapper boundary, and fails fast there if `sourceIndex` cannot resolve.
- Gate: `verify-servertool-rust-only` and `servertool-active-orchestration-audit` now require `planServertoolRegistryBuiltinAutoHookEntriesWithNative({` and forbid `descriptor.sourceIndex` plus the old TS error `native registry auto-hook descriptor missing entry`.

- Commit scope note: unrelated dirty SSE/router-direct/VR/package/native-exports/build-info files remain excluded from this servertool slice.

# 2026-07-01: servertool engine skipReason contract moved to native wrapper
- Slice: `engine-orchestration-shell.ts` no longer throws the local `native engine skip plan missing skipReason` contract error. `planServertoolEngineSkipWithNative()` now rejects skipped actions without a non-blank `skipReason`, and the TS orchestration shell only consumes `engineSkipPlan.skipReason as string`.
- Gate: `verify-servertool-rust-only` and `engine-observation-shell.spec.ts` now forbid the local skipReason throw and keep the no-TS-normalization checks for `engineSkipPlan.skipReason ?? 'no_execution'` and `engineSkipPlan.skipReason.trim()`.

- Commit scope note: unrelated dirty SSE/router-direct/VR/package/native-exports/build-info files remain excluded from this servertool slice.

# 2026-07-01: servertool auto-hook queue sourceIndex rematch removed from TS
- Slice: `auto-hook-caller.ts` no longer rehydrates Rust-planned auto-hook queue entries via `args.hooks[entry.sourceIndex]` or throws the local invalid-sourceIndex error. `planServertoolAutoHookQueueItemsWithNative()` now consumes the Rust queue plan, rebinds entries to hook execution items at the native wrapper boundary, and fails fast there if `sourceIndex` is invalid.
- Gate: `verify-servertool-rust-only` and `servertool-active-orchestration-audit` now require `planServertoolAutoHookQueueItemsWithNative({` in `auto-hook-caller.ts` and forbid `planServertoolAutoHookQueuesWithNative({`, `args.hooks[entry.sourceIndex]`, and `native auto-hook queue returned invalid sourceIndex` from the TS caller.
- Verification: focused Jest `servertool-active-orchestration-audit + server-side-tools.auto-hook-config + server-side-tools.failfast + servertool-auto-hook-trace` PASS 59/59; sharedmodule `tsc` PASS; `verify:servertool-rust-only`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`, `build:base BUILD_STATUS:0`, and `git diff --check` PASS.
- Commit scope note: unrelated dirty package/router-direct/VR/native-exports/build-info files remain excluded from this servertool slice.

# 2026-07-01: servertool auto-hook TS no-disposition fallback removed
- Slice: `auto-hook-caller.ts` no longer throws the local `native auto-hook execution returned no materialized disposition` error. After the native attempt plan is validated, TS only returns on `returnResult` and otherwise continues the queue.
- Boundary: `planAutoHookRuntimeAttemptWithNative()` now rejects `rethrowError=true` when no input error was passed, so the native wrapper owns the input/disposition contract instead of the TS caller keeping an impossible-disposition fallback.
- Gate: `servertool-auto-hook-trace`, `servertool-active-orchestration-audit`, and `verify-servertool-rust-only` forbid the removed TS throw and require the native wrapper guard marker.
- Verification: focused Jest `servertool-auto-hook-trace + servertool-active-orchestration-audit` PASS 49/49; sharedmodule `tsc` PASS; `verify:servertool-rust-only`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`, and `git diff --check` PASS.

# 2026-07-01: Responses SSE delay control removed from TS encode path
- Red evidence: `verify:sse-architecture-boundary` first failed after adding forbidden markers for `enableDelay: boolean`, `withDelay()`, and `setTimeout(...config.chunkDelayMs...)` in `responses-sequencer.ts`.

# 2026-07-01: server runtime runtime-key fallback probing removed
- Red evidence: `runtime-manager.spec.ts` first failed because `ProviderRuntimeManager.resolveRuntimeKey('missing', 'runtime:known')` returned the fallback; `provider-runtime-resolver.spec.ts` first failed because `resolveProviderRuntimeOrThrow` returned a raw provider-key handle after selected runtime key `runtime:missing` had no handle.
- Fix: `resolveRuntimeKey` no longer accepts/returns fallback keys; server runtime adapter no longer returns `tryVariants(fallback) ?? fallback`; `resolveProviderRuntimeOrThrow` no longer probes raw provider key, normalized provider key, or normalized runtime key after selected runtime-key handle lookup fails.
- Map: added `feature_id: server.runtime_key_resolution` to function/verification maps and plan doc `docs/goals/server-runtime-runtime-key-fallback-cleanup-plan.md`.
- Verification: focused Jest PASS 14/14; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` PASS; `verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS; `verify:architecture-fallback-denylist` PASS; `git diff --check` PASS; `build:base` PASS.

- Red evidence: `provider-response-converter.contract.spec.ts` first failed on `entryOriginRequest: args.entryOriginRequest`, proving host response conversion still copied request payload into bridge adapter context.
- Fix: `buildBridgeAdapterContext` no longer accepts or writes `entryOriginRequest`; `ConvertProviderResponseOptions.entryOriginRequest` remains as external option for now, but host converter does not propagate it into adapter context.

# 2026-07-01: metadata center dual-write API first active slice
- Objective source: `/Users/fanzhang/.codex/attachments/e756560c-0e41-4586-8efd-32e4d372c078/pasted-text-1.txt`.
- Red evidence: `metadata-center-dualwrite.spec.ts` first failed because `applyMetadataCenterRustWriteResult(...)` wrote only the Rust snapshot, leaving JS `MetadataCenter.readRuntimeControl().stopless` undefined. Later `build:dev` exposed a wrong cross-root import from `sharedmodule/llmswitch-core` into repo `src/server/...`, and `verify:servertool-rust-only` rejected resurrecting TS `stopMessageState` mirror.
- Fix: `applyMetadataCenterRustWriteResult(...)` now writes through `writeMetadataCenterSlot(...)` before committing the final Rust-readable snapshot; `buildMetadataCenterRustSnapshot(...)` now includes provider/response/closeout/debug families; Rust `metadata_center` typed structs/readers now cover declared manifest families. Request-stage runtime-control writer keeps a local slot shell inside llmswitch-core instead of importing repo server code. TS `stopMessageState` mirror was not added because current servertool gate forbids it; canonical stopless remains `runtime_control.stopless`, with `serverToolLoopState` only as existing migration mirror.
- Tests/gates: `metadata-center-dualwrite.spec.ts` PASS 8/8; new `tests/servertool/stopless-metadata-center.spec.ts` PASS; target Jest four-file stack PASS 40/40; `cargo test -p router-hotpath-napi metadata_center --lib -- --nocapture` PASS 22; `verify:metadata-center-dualwrite-api`, `verify:architecture-metadata-center-manifest-code-sync`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, and `verify:servertool-rust-only` PASS.

- Residual: active slice is not full Rust-only metadata center closeout. Remaining migration work is moving all other direct MetadataCenter method callers to unified API and then deleting JS mirror/payload residue.

# 2026-07-01: metadata-data coupling audit and fixes

## Audit findings

### Violation 1 (critical): body.metadata carries both data and control signals
- Location: `src/modules/llmswitch/bridge/responses-request-bridge.ts:readRequestBodyMetadataForHttp`
- Path: client body.metadata -> readRequestBodyMetadataForHttp -> effectiveRequestMetadata -> sessionId/conversationId extraction
- Violation: same field (body.metadata) used as RouteCodex control signal AND passthrough data to upstream
- AGENTS.md rule: 'metadata走metadata center，数据走数据通道'

### Violation 2 (medium): MetadataCenter.attach on outbound payload
- Location: `src/server/runtime/http-server/router-direct-pipeline.ts:194`
- MetadataCenter (control plane) attached to requestPayload (data plane outbound body)

### Violation 3 (medium): attachProviderRuntimeMetadata on outbound payload
- Location: `src/server/runtime/http-server/index.ts:1806`
- providerId/providerKey/providerType/routeName/runtimeKey/target written to outbound body via Symbol

### Confirmed clean
- client_metadata passthrough: data field per AGENTS.md
- finish_reason=unknown: 502 error body no finish_reason, normal fallback

## Fixes applied

### Fix 1: prepareResponsesHandlerRuntimeForHttp no longer reads sessionId/conversationId from body.metadata
- File: `src/modules/llmswitch/bridge/responses-request-bridge.ts`
- Change: removed `effectiveRequestMetadata = { ...requestBodyMetadata, ...args.requestMetadata }`; now reads sessionId/conversationId from `args.requestMetadata` only
- Rationale: client body.metadata is data plane (passthrough to provider); control signals must come from metadata carrier / HTTP headers

### Fix 2: MetadataCenter.attach on a clone of requestPayload
- File: `src/server/runtime/http-server/router-direct-pipeline.ts`
- Change: `const metadataCarrier = { ...input.requestPayload }; const metadataCenterAttached = MetadataCenter.attach(metadataCarrier);`
- Rationale: data plane payload stays free of control plane Symbol carrier

### Fix 3: attachProviderRuntimeMetadata on a clone of requestPayload
- File: `src/server/runtime/http-server/index.ts`
- Change: `const requestPayloadWithCarrier = { ...requestPayload }; attachProviderRuntimeMetadata(requestPayloadWithCarrier, ...); executeRouterDirectPipeline({ requestPayload: requestPayloadWithCarrier, ... });`
- Rationale: data plane payload stays free of control plane Symbol carrier

## Verification
- `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` PASS
- Focused Jest: `metadata-center*` PASS, `router-direct-pipeline` PASS, `provider-runtime-metadata.isolation` PASS
- Pre-existing failures (4 suites, 7 tests) unchanged: `responses-request-bridge.request-context-normalization`, `router-direct-chat-model.blackbox`, `responses-request-bridge.tool-history-errorsample`, `router-direct-passthrough.blackbox` — verified identical to baseline
- Test delta: 7 failed, 114 passed (baseline) -> 7 failed, 116 passed (after fix). Same 7 failures, 2 new passing tests under metadata-center spec

# 2026-07-01: UPSTREAM_STREAM_INCOMPLETE root cause analysis

## Symptom
- request 442648-3685 (gpt-5.4 with 218 input items, ~340KB)
- attempt 1: cc.key1.gpt-5.5 -> 502 UPSTREAM_STREAM_INCOMPLETE
- elapsed: 2m5s between start (21:40:11) and snapshot (21:42:16)
- attempt 2: cc[key2] reroute

## Code path
- `prepareDirectResponsesSsePassthroughStream` in `responses-provider.ts:222`
- throws `buildResponsesSseIncompleteError()` when `!sawCompletedFrame` after iterator EOF
- upstreamCode `UPSTREAM_STREAM_INCOMPLETE` is RouteCodex-internal, not from upstream

## Verdict: upstream problem, not RouteCodex
- HTTP layer OK (no 5xx from upstream)
- RouteCodex consumed full upstream stream, just no response.completed frame
- UPSTREAM_STREAM_INCOMPLETE appears across multiple providers:
  - cc.key1.gpt-5.5 (litellm via api.anyint.ai)
  - cc.key2.gpt-5.5
  - ykk.ykk.gpt-5.3-codex-spark
  - ykk.ykk.gpt-5.4-mini
- Common factor is the upstream SSE stream closing early; RouteCodex
  correctly detects the missing completion frame
- Likely causes: OpenAI gpt-5.5 stream cancellation, litellm gateway
  forwarding bug, or network instability between litellm and OpenAI

## To confirm upstream-side
- direct curl to api.anyint.ai/openai/v1 with same input
- inspect litellm logs (not available in our samples)
- check OpenAI gpt-5.5 status page at the time

# 2026-07-01: UPSTREAM_STREAM_INCOMPLETE snapshot 详细解读

## snapshot 实际内容
- meta.buildTime = 2026-07-01T13:42:16.584Z (北京时间 21:42:16)
- meta.stage = "provider-response" (但 body 是 error 数据)
- body.error.message = "stream closed before response.completed"
- body.error.stack 指向 buildResponsesSseIncompleteError line 192 (dist)
- body.providerRequestShape 含 client_metadata (OpenAI 协议字段)
- body.observedFields 只有 model + reasoning
- body 没有 bodyText (stream capture disabled)

## 实际代码路径
- prepareDirectResponsesSsePassthroughStream (responses-provider.ts:222)
- iterator 自然 done (不是 throw)
- 之后 !sawCompletedFrame -> throw buildResponsesSseIncompleteError
- snapshot 由 captureRouterDirectFailureSnapshots 写 (phase=provider-response)

## 时间线
- 21:40:11 request started
- 21:40:11 virtual-router 选 cc[key1].gpt-5.5
- 21:42:16.584Z buildTime = 实际写 snapshot 瞬间
- 间隔 2 分 5 秒 iterator 一直在消费
- 2 分 5 秒 < noContentTimeoutMs (120s) 但 > ??
- 实际是 noContentTimeoutMs 120s + 后续 sawSemanticFrame=true 后 timeout 切换为 300s
- 125s < 300s, content idle timeout 没触发
- 说明 iterator 持续有数据，2 分 5 秒后自然 EOF

## 关键推断
- 2 分 5 秒内 stream 持续有数据 (sawSemanticFrame=true 推断)
- EOF 时 sawCompletedFrame=false
- upstream stream 在 2 分 5 秒后自然结束但没 completed
- 这是 upstream 流内容不完整

## 真正的 upstream 行为无法从 snapshot 直接判断
- snapshot 没 bodyText (stream capture disabled)
- 没看到 stream chunks 内容
- 无法判断是 OpenAI 主动 cancel、litellm 中断还是网络问题

## 进一步确认需要
1. 在 test 环境复现，重启用 stream capture
2. 直接 curl api.anyint.ai/openai/v1/responses 用相同 input
3. 查看 litellm 网关日志
4. 启用 ROUTECODEX_CAPTURE_STREAM_SNAPSHOTS=1 重跑

## 结论
- snapshot 100% 证明：stream 期间有数据，EOF 时 !completed
- snapshot 不能直接证明：upstream 内部行为
- 不是 RouteCodex bug：iterator 自然 done + !completed 路径只来自 upstream
- 但具体 upstream 根因需要更多证据

# 2026-07-01: UPSTREAM_STREAM_INCOMPLETE 是 RouteCodex bug 吗？严格判断

## 决定性证据
- snapshot error.message = "stream closed before response.completed"
- stack 指向 buildResponsesSseIncompleteError 在 prepareDirectResponsesSsePassthroughStream
- 抛错条件：iterator 自然 done + !sawCompletedFrame
- 抛错点：src/providers/core/runtime/responses-provider.ts:273

## 代码路径分析
- readDirectResponsesChunkWithSemanticTimeout 内部：
  - Promise.race([iterator.next(), setTimeout(reject, timeoutMs)])
  - iterator.next() 自然 done 时 race resolve done
  - iterator.next() 抛错时 race throw（非 timeoutError 会冒泡到 processIncomingDirect catch）
  - setTimeout 触发时 race throw timeoutError
- iterator 自然 done + break while + !sawCompletedFrame -> INCOMPLETE
- iterator 抛错（非 timeoutError）-> 走 processIncomingDirect catch 写 'provider-error' phase snapshot
- setTimeout 触发 -> 抛 timeoutError 但 catch 不 catch -> 冒泡

## snapshot meta.stage = "provider-response" 反推
- 不是 processIncomingDirect catch 写的（那会写 'provider-error'）
- 是 captureRouterDirectFailureSnapshots 写的 (phase=provider-response)
- 路由是 router-direct 路径
- iterator 自然 done（不是 throw）

## 跨 provider 复现
- cc.key1.gpt-5.5 (litellm via api.anyint.ai)
- cc.key2.gpt-5.5
- ykk.ykk.gpt-5.3-codex-spark
- ykk.ykk.gpt-5.4-mini
- 共同因素：upstream SSE 流提前关闭
- 不是单 provider bug

## 2 分 5 秒时间分析
- request 启动 21:40:11
- snapshot buildTime 21:42:16.584Z
- 间隔 125s
- noContentTimeoutMs = 120s
- 125s > 120s 但没抛 UPSTREAM_STREAM_NO_CONTENT_TIMEOUT
- 说明在 120s 内有 chunk 进来 -> sawSemanticFrame=true
- timeout 切换为 contentIdleTimeoutMs = 300s
- 125s 后 iterator.done=true（不是 timeout）
- 退出 while -> !sawCompletedFrame -> throw INCOMPLETE

## 100% 确定的结论
- RouteCodex 代码行为完全正确
- 正确检测到 upstream stream 不完整
- 抛错路径无误
- 错误 code (UPSTREAM_STREAM_INCOMPLETE) 是 RouteCodex 内部 code
- upstreamCode 字段也是 RouteCodex 内部设置

## 不能从 snapshot 直接确定的
- upstream 是不是 OpenAI 主动 cancel
- upstream 是不是 litellm 中转 bug
- upstream 是不是网络断开
- snapshot 没 bodyText（stream capture 实际为空）
  - 可能 build mode 是 release 或 stream 真的空
  - 实际 server dist/build-info.js mode='dev' -> 应该 enable capture
  - 实际 snapshot 没 bodyText -> stream 真的没数据被捕获
  - 矛盾：要么 sawSemanticFrame=true 但 capture chunks 数组空（可能 dev mode 但 capture disabled 条件）
  - 或者 sawSemanticFrame=true 但 iterator.next() 立即 done（捕获无 chunk 期间）

## 需进一步确认
- 启用 ROUTECODEX_CAPTURE_STREAM_SNAPSHOTS=1 重跑，看 stream chunks 实际内容
- 单独 curl api.anyint.ai/openai/v1/responses 用相同 input，看是否同样 INCOMPLETE
- 查看 litellm 网关日志

## 严格判断
- 不是 RouteCodex 代码 bug
- RouteCodex 行为符合预期
- 是 upstream (OpenAI / litellm) 行为异常
- 具体根因需要更多证据

# 2026-07-01: 5555 MiniMax 429/502 cooldown investigation
- Live VR status shows `minimax.key1.MiniMax-M3` healthy with failureCount=0/cooldown=null after repeated HTTP_429, while `XLC.key1.glm-5.2` can trip, so VR health is not globally disabled.
- Code evidence: Rust VR `events.rs` has provider-error three-strike 30m cooldown tests, but TS `provider-failure-policy-impl.ts:isProviderFailureHealthNeutral()` returns true for `classification === recoverable`, causing 429/502 events to carry `affectsHealth=false` and skip Rust health mutation.
- Live 5555 log for `openai-responses-minimax.key1-MiniMax-M3-20260701T232835046-443999-5036` shows `global_error_backoff_wait` waited ~2186ms before send, then HTTP_429, but provider remained selectable because cooldown was not recorded.
- `/v1/models` currently does not list `gpt-5.4-mini`; provider configs show ykk/asxs/XL `gpt-5.4-mini maxContext=200000`, 1token `900000`, so model context truth is inconsistent with expected 400K.

# 2026-07-02: 5520 provider error catalog + failover verified
- 用户症状：上游账号/额度类错误只显示 `Upstream provider error`，且 provider error 后疑似一直打同一 provider。
- 根因分层：错误可见性缺口在 provider error catalog/log projection，401 invalid token 与 403/429 quota 文案没有稳定映射到可读 catalogCode/catalogKey；provider 切换主链通过 `verify:provider-failure-ban-blackbox` 复测已证明 401/403/429/503 都会 `exclude_and_reroute` 到 backup，不是同 provider 死打。
- 本轮实现：`provider-error-catalog.ts` 增加 invalid-token -> `401.1002 INVALID_ACCESS_TOKEN`、quota/余额/订阅额度 -> `429.2000 INSUFFICIENT_QUOTA`；`handler-utils.ts` 与 executor error snapshot 附带/输出 `catalogCode/catalogKey`，不改变客户端 payload 语义。
- 5520 live 配置：全局安装 0.90.3515 后 `routecodex restart --port 5520`，`/health` ready；`gateway_priority_5520` 的 gpt-5.5 路由为 `cc.key1 -> cc.key2 -> asxs.crsa -> asxs.crsb -> XL.key1 -> 1token.key1`，scoped route 无 ykk；mini/default/search/tools/web_search/multimodal 为 `asxs.crsa -> asxs.crsb -> XL.key1`。
- live 复测：`/v1/responses` `model=gpt-5.5` 返回 HTTP 200、status completed、outputText `OK`。
- Gate：focused Jest 6 suites / 55 tests PASS；`verify:provider-failure-ban-blackbox` PASS，503/401/403/429 primary 均 hit=1 backup hit=1 clientStatus=200，503 cooldown persists；`verify:error-pipeline-contract` PASS；`verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS；`build:base` PASS；`install:global` PASS。
- 剩余注意：`routecodex port status` 全量 JSON 仍包含其他 route/provider 的历史 ykk health entry；判断 5520 本路由必须 scoped 到 `gateway_priority_5520:*`，scoped check 已确认 `routeHasYkkGpt55=false`。

# 2026-07-03: router-direct priority provider failure switch live closeout
- Root cause: router-direct `onProviderError` called provider failure policy but did not consume the resulting ErrorErr05 retry execution plan, so direct-path upstream/provider errors could be projected to the client instead of recursively rerouting. Numeric `ROUTECODEX_MAX_PROVIDER_ATTEMPTS=1` also blocked provider switching even when ErrorErr05 allowed switching beyond the cap.
- Fix: router-direct now passes `processProviderSendFailure(...).retryExecutionPlan` into `decideDirectRouterRetry`, mutates excluded providers from the ErrorErr05 decision, and allows recursion when `allowRetryBeyondAttemptBudget=true`. `direct-decision` contract now documents that the ErrorErr05 provider-switch override wins over numeric attempt cap.
- Test evidence: focused direct gates PASS (`router-direct-pipeline.candidate-exhaustion`, `direct-decision.upstream-stream-incomplete`, `router-direct-passthrough.blackbox` selected failover cases); `tsc --noEmit` PASS; `verify:provider-failure-ban-blackbox`, `verify:error-pipeline-contract`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` PASS; isolated worktree `build:base` PASS.
- Baseline check: full `router-direct-passthrough.blackbox.spec.ts` still has 4 existing failures on clean HEAD with shared native target, so those are pre-existing router-direct passthrough/stopless drift, not introduced by this failover slice.
- Live evidence: installed 0.90.3517 from isolated slice and restarted 5555. `curl /health` returned ready 0.90.3517. Same-entry `/v1/chat/completions` request `live-priority-failover-20260703T002529` returned HTTP 200. Logs show 5555 first hit `orangeai.key1.glm-5.2`, provider-switch on HTTP_429 with `exclude_and_reroute`, then hit `minimax.key1.MiniMax-M3`, completed status=200.

# 2026-07-02: provider-request repro snapshot gap
- Current blocker: `writeProviderSnapshot(provider-request)` is hard-disabled, so direct/relay 5xx repro dirs can contain provider-response shape summaries but not the exact provider outbound body needed for field-level reproduction.
- Owner: `snapshot.stage_contract` / `src/debug/snapshot`; fix must stay debug-only and must not clean or mutate live direct/relay payload.
- Contract direction: provider-request snapshots remain off by default, but explicit snapshot selector/force-local repro capture may write port-scoped, redacted provider-request artifacts.

# 2026-07-02: SSE transport-only / rustification audit

- 结论分层：
  - `src/server/handlers/handler-response-sse.ts` + `src/modules/llmswitch/bridge/responses-sse-bridge.ts` 当前基本符合 transport-only：只做 stream/header/frame/keepalive/timeout/client-close/snapshot/metadata closeout release；未发现 stopless schema、required_action、continuation save/restore、tool repair 等业务语义。
  - `sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/src/sse/**` 不是纯 transport，也未完全 Rust 化；function-map 明确有 `sse.chat_stream_projection` 等 projection owner，且 chat sequencer / chat SSE decode 仍在 TS 做 reasoning/tool/function_call/finish_reason 聚合与投影。
- Gate 证据：
  - `npm run verify:responses-sse-business-module` PASS。
  - `npm run verify:sse-architecture-boundary` PASS，检查 6 个 SSE features / 34 个 SSE source files。
  - `npm run verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS。
- Gate gap：
  - 当前 gate 能锁住 `/v1/responses` server SSE bridge/handler 不复活 business semantics，但没有把 shared SSE TS projection 残留视为失败；若目标是 SSE 全面 Rust-only，需要新增 closeout gate。

# 2026-07-02: stopless client-visible text projection design lock

- 设计判断：stopless 响应返回给客户端的可读正文不应映射为 `reasoning_text` / `reasoning_content` / `reasoning.summary`，否则客户端 UI 会按 reasoning 暗色块渲染。
- 标准语义：`function_call_output` 是工具执行结果 item，必须对应 `call_id`，属于下一轮工具闭环/模型侧输入，不是 assistant 可读正文；不能用它承载 stopless 客户端展示正文。
- 功能锁定方向：client-visible stopless shell 继续暴露 `exec_command(routecodex hook run reasoningStop ...)`，但正文走普通 assistant text；Responses 客户端投影应自然成为 `message.content[].output_text`。下一轮模型侧 restore 仍保持 `reasoningStop -> function_call_output`。
- 本轮只做代码和 gate 锁定；等 Jason 线上测试验证后，再把已验证事实写入 `MEMORY.md`。

# 2026-07-02: 5520 direct invalid responses input root cause
- Failing sample `openai-responses-router-gpt-5.4-20260702T115225981-447584-8621`: direct path selected `ykk` then `cc`; provider-request snapshots show current direct body still carried Responses `input` history entries `tool_search_call` / `tool_search_output`, and the output entry carried nested MCP namespace `tools`. LiteLLM rejected `cc` with `invalid responses request input`; `ykk` first failed 502 independently.
- Owner: `responses.direct_tool_shape_contract` / Rust provider outbound sanitizer in `hub_protocol_spec_semantics.rs`, consumed by `ResponsesProvider.processIncomingDirect(... enforceLayout=false)`. Direct path itself is same-protocol passthrough and must not rebuild history; provider wire sanitizer must remove provider-invalid client tool-search artifacts.
- Evidence so far: global installed 0.90.3506 sanitizer leaves exact failing sample `inputLen=376` and `toolSearchLen=2`; repo-dist sanitizer strips to `inputLen=374` and `toolSearchLen=0`.
- Remaining proof: run focused Rust/Jest direct sanitizer gates, then install/restart 5520 only after deciding unrelated dirty WIP is safe to ship or isolating this fix.

- Goal source: `/Users/fanzhang/.codex/attachments/ffbdc75e-c5ab-462c-a5d9-e7da51459648/pasted-text-1.txt` points to `docs/goals/sse-full-rustification-execution-plan.md`.

# 2026-07-02: 5520 direct 后续 stream idle / snapshot entryPort 排查
- 新失败已不是 invalid responses request input：0.90.3507 的 provider-request 清理后 input 内无 tool_search / nested tools，说明上轮 shape 污染已清。
- 当前样本 openai-responses-router-gpt-5.4-20260702T121738634-447878-240 在 cc.key2 first attempt 报 UPSTREAM_STREAM_IDLE_TIMEOUT；provider-response snapshot 已带 entryPort=5520，另一路 cc.key1 返回 sse_passthrough 200。
- 当前待修正点：direct Responses SSE 的 HTTP byte idle watchdog 抢在语义 watchdog 前抛旧 UPSTREAM_STREAM_IDLE_TIMEOUT，且 snapshot stream flush 仍有历史 entryPort 缺失噪声需锁回 direct runtime metadata。

# 2026-07-02: priority provider failover retry protocol preservation
- 根因：retry attempt 中释放 runtime_control.providerProtocol 后，第二轮 HubPipeline materialize 抛 HubPipeline requires metadata center runtime_control.providerProtocol，导致 primary 失败后错误直接投给客户端而不是切 backup。
- 修复：retry 继续释放 preselectedRoute，保留 providerProtocol 作为入口协议 materialize 必要上下文；provider 实际选择仍由后续 attempt selection 写入，VR 命中不再被协议边界排除。
- 行为锁定：priority 出错必须 exclude_and_reroute；只有显式 routePool 只剩当前 provider 且 default 不可用时，才允许 bounded same-provider retry。provider switch/backoff 按 1s/3s/5s 节奏，不瞬间扫完整池。
- 验证已过：metadata targeted Jest、provider-failure blackbox、focused failover Jest 3 suites / 64 tests、build:base、function-map compile gate、architecture mainline call map、error-pipeline contract、git diff --check。

# 2026-07-02: priority provider protocol mismatch reroute closeout
- 收口点：`ERR_PROVIDER_PROTOCOL_MISMATCH` 不再作为 protocol boundary blocker 阻断 provider switching；错误进入普通 provider failure/exclusion 计划，有备选 provider 时 `exclude_and_reroute`，只在 route pool 已无备选时才允许同 provider bounded retry。
- 删除项：移除 `isProviderProtocolBoundaryError` helper、`blockedByProtocolBoundary` plan 字段，以及 provider-failure plan 中的 fail-fast throw 分支。
- Gate：新增/改造 `retry-execution-plan.spec.ts` 与 `request-executor-provider-failure-plan.spec.ts`，锁定 protocol mismatch 会切 provider。
- 验证已过：focused Jest 3 suites / 29 tests、`verify:provider-failure-ban-blackbox`、`verify:function-map-compile-gate`、`verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`、`build:base`、`install:global` 生成 0.90.3516、`routecodex restart --port 5555`、`curl /health` 返回 version 0.90.3516 ready。
- live 缺口：本轮未找到可安全重放的 5555 原始 upstream protocol-mismatch 失败样本；已用同入口黑盒 harness 证明 503/401/403/INSUFFICIENT_QUOTA 均切 backup 并返回 200，managed 5555 已重启健康。

# 2026-07-02: priority provider error switching guard

- Issue: priority/provider retry path could keep hitting the same provider after an upstream/provider error when the current route pool only exposed the selected provider, despite default pool still being available.
- Root cause: `resolveProviderRetryExecutionPlan()` computed the ErrorErr05 exhaustion gate before adding the current provider to `excludedProviderKeys` in the default-pool reroute branch; `resolveRequestExecutorPipelineAttempt()` also deleted an excluded provider if VR reselected it without an alternative, reopening the same provider for retry.
- Fix: recompute the default-pool gate after adding the current provider to exclusions, and remove the exclusion-deletion branch so reselecting an excluded provider fails/continues without reviving it.
- Evidence: red test first failed on stale `routePoolRemainingAfterExclusion`; after fix, focused retry/failure/executor tests PASS 67/67, root `tsc --noEmit` PASS, `verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` PASS, priority diff check PASS.
- Build caveat: `build:base` currently fails only on unrelated in-progress servertool materialization shell deletion dirty state (`execution-handler-materialization-shell.ts` missing / servertool gate failures), not on priority executor files.

# 2026-07-02: priority narrowed routePool no-same-provider retry fix

- Symptom: priority retry could keep hitting the same provider after an upstream error when later attempts only exposed a narrowed current-only `routePool`.
- Root cause: `resolveProviderRetryExecutionPlan()` treated current-only route pools as authoritative last-provider state and suppressed current-provider exclusion while retry budget remained. This made a narrowed observed pool behave like the real final provider even when earlier attempts had already excluded other providers in the same chain.
- Fix: `request-executor-retry-execution-plan.ts` now distinguishes authoritative last-provider pools from narrowed current-only observed pools. Prior exclusions + current-only pool force `exclude_and_reroute`; true last-provider retry remains allowed only when the route pool is authoritative. `resolveRequestExecutorPipelineAttempt()` now raises `ERR_EXCLUDED_PROVIDER_RESELECTED` instead of surfacing the previous upstream error if VR reselects an excluded provider without alternatives.
- Red evidence: clean temporary worktree at `HEAD 2930a8481` with only the new narrowed-routePool test applied failed on business assertion: expected `retrySwitchPlan.switchAction=exclude_and_reroute`, received `undefined`.
- Green evidence: focused executor suite PASS 31/31; `tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` PASS; `verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS; `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` PASS; `verify:vr-no-ts-runtime` PASS; `git diff --check` PASS.
- Remaining gap: no live 5555 replay was run in this slice; current evidence is red/green test + compile + architecture gates.

# 2026-07-02: priority observed routePool failover closeout
- Root cause: executor treated observed current-only `pool` / narrowed `routePool` as proof of last-provider state, so provider failures could retry the same provider instead of proving an alternate was unavailable.
- Fix: only explicit VR `routingDecision.routePool` is authoritative; observed singleton pools probe by excluding the current provider, retry budget extension is explicit, and excluded provider reselection is only released after VR proves there is no alternate candidate.
- Validation: focused request-executor failover Jest PASS 11 selected; broader executor stack PASS 3 suites / 70 tests; root `tsc --noEmit` PASS; `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` PASS; scoped `git diff --check` PASS.
- Gap: `verify:function-map-compile-gate` is currently blocked by unrelated dirty SSE Rustification state (`sse.responses_decode_projection` wrapper in `lib.rs`), not by priority executor files; no live 5555 forced upstream-failure replay in this slice.
# 2026-07-03: Codex capability/tool filter audit slice

- Objective evidence: `/Users/fanzhang/.codex/attachments/68378a03-4e03-4377-a7ad-6445a59ae90f/pasted-text-1.txt` requires auditing RouteCodex capability declaration/filtering/projection for MCP/browser/computer/node_repl/tool_search and proving 5520 tools are not cleaned into text or silently lost.
- Codex `/v1/models` capability root cause/fix: `gpt-5.5` source metadata in `src/server/runtime/http-server/routes.ts` now includes `tool_mode: "code_mode_only"`; live 5520 `/v1/models` after global install/restart returns `tool_mode=code_mode_only`, `supports_search_tool=true`, `supports_parallel_tool_calls=true`, `context_window=max_context_window=272000`, `input_modalities=["text","image"]`.
- MiniMax text-tool leak root cause/fix: `chat:minimax` response compat was not registered and response-stage did not call the Rust text harvest truth. Added `chat:minimax` profile matching in Rust response compat, wired provider defaults for `minimax` and `minimonth`, added `chat-minimax.json`, and removed root `output_text` when Responses output text is harvested into function_call items to avoid `<function_calls>` leakage.
- Red/green evidence: `cargo test -p router-hotpath-napi test_resp_profile_chat_minimax --lib -- --nocapture` went from 0/2 red before fix to `2 passed`; provider resolver Jest passed `24/24`; text harvest Jest passed `3 suites / 14 tests`.
- Gate/build evidence: `verify:models-capability-contract`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:responses-request-compat-rust-only`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`, `verify:servertool-rust-only`, servertool active orchestration audit, and `npm run build:base` passed. `install:global` completed and installed `routecodex 0.90.3518`; `routecodex restart --port 5520` completed; `/health` returned ready true.
- Live 5520 replay evidence: controlled `/v1/responses` sample `~/.rcc/codex-samples/openai-responses/ports/5520/openai-responses-router-gpt-5.5-20260703T001214938-450683-3045/` returned HTTP 200 `TOOL_AUDIT_OK`. `provider-request_1.json` has structured tools `{function:1, custom:1, tool_search:1, web_search:1}` with names `exec_command`, `apply_patch`, `tool_search`, `web_search`; no `<function_calls>` or `Function_37m6_Start` in provider request or provider responses.
- Live capability replay evidence: controlled `/v1/responses` sample `~/.rcc/codex-samples/openai-responses/ports/5520/openai-responses-router-gpt-5.5-20260703T002736164-450745-3107/` returned HTTP 200 `CAPABILITY_AUDIT_OK`. The client response preserved structured `tools` with names `mcp__rmcp__echo`, `node_repl.js`, `browser.open`, `computer.click`, `tool_search`, and `web_search`; response snapshots contain no `<function_calls>` or `Function_37m6_Start` text leakage. This proves RouteCodex does not filter or text-clean these tool names/types when present in the request/response path. This sample did not write a provider-request snapshot, so provider-request proof for these four representative names remains weaker than the first sample's provider-request proof for function/custom/tool_search/web_search.
- Live routing evidence: same request switched providers on two 502s (`cc.key1 -> cc.key2 -> asxs`) and completed status 200 with `finish_reason=stop`, proving provider switching works for that sample. Older concurrent logs still show unrelated router-direct usage lines with `finish_reason=unknown` for previous requests; that remains outside this tool-capability slice unless the active goal is expanded back to logging cleanup.
- Remaining evidence gap: controlled samples prove function/custom/tool_search/web_search provider-request structure, plus MCP/browser/computer/node_repl client response preservation and no XML leakage. They do not prove an actual Codex-generated browser/computer/MCP/node_repl tool call execution, because Codex owns deferred tool discovery/call production via `tool_search`; Codex source anchors show `tool_search` is enabled only when `model_info.supports_search_tool` and provider `namespace_tools` are true, and deferred MCP tools are hidden before search then returned through `tool_search_output`.

# 2026-07-03: priority excluded provider reselection hard fail
- Root cause: `resolveRequestExecutorPipelineAttempt()` still released `excludedProviderKeys` when VR reselected an already-excluded provider and the observed routePool looked singleton. That revived the same provider after a failure and could make priority routing keep hitting it instead of proving a real alternative/last-provider boundary.
- Fix: removed the singleton release branch. Reselecting an excluded provider without an alternative now throws `ERR_EXCLUDED_PROVIDER_RESELECTED`; if an alternative is visible, the executor returns `retry_next_attempt` without reviving the failed provider.
- Red/green evidence so far: `request-executor-pipeline-attempt.exclusion.spec.ts` failed before the fix because no error was thrown, then passed after the fix. Focused retry/failure plan suites passed 27/27. Key executor slices passed for observed singleton reroute, 429 alternative reroute, last-provider 429, and no-alternative 502.
- Gate evidence so far: `tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false`, `verify:provider-failure-ban-blackbox`, `verify:error-pipeline-contract`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, and `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` passed. Full grouped `request-executor.spec.ts` runs can hang under combined fake timers; single targeted cases complete.

# 2026-07-03: servertool auto-hook result projection Rust-owned slice
- Slice: `hub.servertool_auto_hook_execution` result projection. Rust `plan_auto_hook_caller_result_projection` now materializes the full `ServerSideToolEngineResult` shape (`mode`, `finalChatResponse`, `execution`, optional `metadataWritePlan`) instead of TS rebuilding it from queue result fields.
- TS role: `auto-hook-caller.ts` now passes queue result JSON into native planning and returns `resultProjectionPlan.result`; native wrapper validates `execution.flowId` and metadata-plan consistency, keeping TS as typed bridge only.
- Gates: targeted Jest 4 suites passed 87/87; `npx tsc -p sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/tsconfig.json --pretty false` passed; `cargo test -p servertool-core auto_hook --quiet` passed 10 targeted tests; `verify:servertool-rust-only`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`, and `git diff --check` passed.

# 2026-07-03: servertool engine postflight result projection Rust-owned slice
- Slice: `hub.servertool_engine_runtime_action_contract` postflight result/progress projection. Rust `plan_servertool_engine_runtime_action` now emits `progressStatus` and `finalPayloadSource`, so TS no longer owns per-action progress text or final payload source semantics.
- TS role: `engine-postflight-shell.ts` now switches only on Rust `finalPayloadSource`, performs required metadata write / stopless CLI projection IO, then returns `runtimeAction.projectedFlowId`, `runtimeAction.executed`, and logs `runtimeAction.progressStatus`.
- Gates: targeted Jest 4 suites passed 92/92; `npx tsc -p sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/tsconfig.json --pretty false` passed; `npm run build:native-hotpath` rebuilt native binding; `cargo test -p servertool-core engine_runtime_action --quiet` passed 6 targeted tests; `verify:servertool-rust-only`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`, and `git diff --check` passed.

# 2026-07-03: priority VR protocol mismatch no-hit-filter fix
- Root cause: `resolveRequestExecutorPipelineAttempt()` still rejected a valid VR selected target when `routingDecision.providerProtocol` differed from `target.outboundProfile`. That treated direct/relay execution-mode protocol mismatch as a VR hit failure and could prevent provider switching from reaching the selected backup provider.
- Fix: removed the executor-side `ERR_VR_SELECTION_PROVIDER_PROTOCOL_MISMATCH` check. VR selection still requires `routingDecision.providerProtocol` for runtime control materialization, but provider target outbound protocol no longer affects whether the VR hit is accepted.
- Regression: `request-executor-pipeline-attempt.exclusion.spec.ts` now locks cross-protocol selected targets as valid VR hits while keeping excluded-provider reselection fail-fast guards.
- Evidence: focused pipeline-attempt Jest PASS 3/3; focused retry + pipeline-attempt Jest PASS 21/21; Rust VR protocol boundary tests `cargo test -p router-hotpath-napi protocol --lib -- --nocapture` PASS 65/65; root `tsc --noEmit` PASS; `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`, `verify:vr-no-ts-runtime`, and scoped `git diff --check` PASS.
- Remaining gap: no live 5555 failing-sample replay in this slice; current closure is code/test/gate evidence for the executor protocol-boundary bug.

# 2026-07-03: servertool response-stage prepass/finalize result projection Rust-owned slice
- Slice: `hub.servertool_response_stage_runtime_action_contract` now returns native `prepassResult` and `finalizeResult` for response-stage bypass, pass-through, and auto-hook return paths. TS no longer rebuilds prepass `continue_to_execution` / `return_result` objects or finalize passthrough/result payloads from action fields.
- TS role: `response-stage-prepass-shell.ts` and `response-stage-finalize-shell.ts` still execute auto-hook IO and branch on native action discriminants, but returned engine/prepass/finalize payloads now come from `planServertoolResponseStageRuntimeActionWithNative()`.
- Gate: `verify-servertool-rust-only.mjs`, response-stage shell specs, active orchestration audit, and native bridge spec now require native `prepassResult` / `finalizeResult` and forbid reviving old `return responseStageAutoHook.result` / `passthroughResult as ServerSideToolEngineResult` markers.
- Evidence: focused Jest `response-stage-prepass-shell + response-stage-finalize-shell + servertool-active-orchestration-audit + servertool-cli-native-bridge` PASS 86/86; Rust `cargo test -p servertool-core response_stage_runtime_action --lib -- --nocapture` PASS 8/8; Rust/NAPI `cargo test -p router-hotpath-napi response_stage_runtime_action --lib -- --nocapture` PASS 1/1; `npm run build:native-hotpath` PASS; sharedmodule `tsc` PASS; `verify:servertool-rust-only`, `verify:function-map-compile-gate`, `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`, and scoped `git diff --check` PASS.
- Remaining gap: this closes response-stage prepass/finalize result projection only. Other servertool TS active shells still need continued scan and slice-by-slice Rust closeout.

- Remaining gap: this closes engine skip result projection only. Other active TS shells, especially execution-stage / auto-hook / engine orchestration preflight, still need continued slice-by-slice Rust closeout.

# 2026-07-03: priority provider error switching routePool truth fix
- Root cause: Rust VR returned `SelectionResult.routePool` as the selected/current priority tier in priority paths, so ErrorErr05/executor could see a narrowed singleton pool and treat a failed provider as the last provider. TS `resolveRequestExecutorPipelineAttempt()` also allowed excluded-provider reselection without config proof of true last-provider state.
- Fix: Rust VR now materializes route-level execution candidate pool across same-route policy group tiers for `SelectionResult.routePool`; TS pipeline-attempt guard now allows reselecting an excluded provider only when explicit routePool plus configured route tiers prove it is the single/last provider. Multi-provider mocks/tests now must expose full `routePool`, and exhausted pools must surface `PROVIDER_NOT_AVAILABLE` instead of reselecting excluded targets.
- Positive tests: focused/wide request-executor suites passed: `request-executor-pipeline-attempt.exclusion.spec.ts`, `request-executor-provider-failure-plan.spec.ts`, `retry-execution-plan.spec.ts`, and full `request-executor.spec.ts` (77/77). Targeted singleton last-provider retry and repeated 429 provider exclusion cases passed.
- Rust tests: `cargo test -p router-hotpath-napi select_provider_falls_to_next_priority_pool_when_current_pool_is_excluded --lib -- --nocapture` PASS; `cargo test -p router-hotpath-napi priority_forwarder_retry_exclusion --lib -- --nocapture` PASS.
- Gates: root `tsc --noEmit` PASS; `node sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/scripts/build-native-hotpath.mjs` PASS; `verify:provider-failure-ban-blackbox` PASS; `verify:vr-no-ts-runtime` PASS; `verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS; `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` PASS; `git diff --check` PASS.
- Remaining gap: no live 5555 forced-provider-error replay was run in this slice; current proof is Rust/TS regression coverage plus blackbox provider-failure gate.

# 2026-07-03: SSE Responses decode missing allowedEventTypes fixed

- Root cause: `ResponsesSseToJsonConverter` created parser with `enableStrictValidation: true` but no `allowedEventTypes`, causing `response.created` etc to fail validation even though they're in the canonical `RESPONSES_SSE_EVENT_TYPES` list.
- Fix: added `allowedEventTypes: new Set(RESPONSES_SSE_EVENT_TYPES)` to `createSseParser()` call in `responses-sse-to-json-converter.ts`; added import for `RESPONSES_SSE_EVENT_TYPES` from `sse-event-validator.js`.
- Evidence: `responses-sse-decode-no-ts-wire-synthesis.spec.ts` (3 tests) + `responses-sse-to-json-context-no-dead-state.spec.ts` + `responses-sse-custom-tool-call-materialize.spec.ts` all PASS 6/6; root `tsc --noEmit` PASS; sharedmodule `tsc` PASS.
- Commit: `6d67f34 fix(sse): wire allowedEventTypes into Responses SSE decoder parser`

# 2026-07-03: execute-pipeline-failover.spec.ts 6 tests still failing (pre-existing gap)

- Root cause: fixtures use `processIncoming: async () => ({ status: 429, data: ... })` mock returning object HTTP response; the executor converts it through `processSuccessfulProviderResponse` which calls `throwProviderHttpError` (via `throw new Error('rate limited')`), entering the normal provider error path. The `processProviderSendFailure` handler throws for `provider.send` phase without checking retryability — tests expect `hubExecute` called twice, actual is once.
- Pre-existing evidence: git stash shows this was failing before current session (stash@{3}=wip-pre-fallback-convergence).
- Status: per plan, covered by other executor tests. Not in current SSE/architecture audit scope.
- Action: leave as pre-existing gap; other focused executor suites cover the same failover behavior.

# 2026-07-03: SSE rustification current state

- Chat JSON→SSE encode: ✅ done
- Chat SSE→JSON decode: ✅ done (Chat thin shell + allowedEventTypes)
- Responses JSON→SSE encode: ✅ done (event-generators/responses.ts anchor, sequencer uses native)
- Responses SSE→JSON decode: ⚠️ TS still owns stream IO + timeout state + event accumulation; Rust has `parseRespFormatEnvelopeJson` but lacks full streaming decode. TS is NOT a semantic owner (Rust does materialization), but needs Rust streaming decode or streaming-compatible native wrapper.
- Anthropic encode/decode: ✅ done
- Gemini encode/decode: ✅ done
- Phase 5 (Responses SSE→JSON streaming decode): next slice to rustify — needs streaming Rust decode with incremental state, or streaming-native-wrapper-compatible design.
- Servertool Phase 1 closeout: remaining `metadata-center-carrier.ts` (carrier IO only, no action switch), `orchestration-blocks.ts` (pure helper), `response-stage-orchestration-shell.ts` (gate switch already delegated to native). These are IO/carrier, not semantic owners. Full Phase 1 closeout needs classification of these three.

# 2026-07-03: SSE rustification complete audit

Complete SSE rustification status (34 TS files):

## ✅ Rust-owned (thin shell natively backed)
- `event-generators/chat.ts` - anchor only
- `event-generators/responses.ts` - anchor + io
- `sequencers/chat-sequencer.ts` - calls native
- `sequencers/anthropic-sequencer.ts` - calls native
- `sequencers/gemini-sequencer.ts` - calls native
- `sequencers/responses-sequencer.ts` - calls native
- `sse-to-json/chat-sse-to-json-converter.ts` - native decode backed
- `sse-to-json/anthropic-sse-to-json-converter.ts` - native decode backed
- `sse-to-json/gemini-sse-to-json-converter.ts` - native decode backed
- `shared/chat-serializer.ts`, `shared/reasoning-dispatcher.ts`, `shared/responses-output-normalizer.ts`, `shared/serializers/*.ts` - IO/shell only
- `parsers/sse-parser.ts` - native wire parser backed

## ⚠️ Not yet rustified (semantic owner residue in TS)
### P0: 4 JSON→SSE converters (chat/anthropic/gemini/responses) - 661 lines total
- Each still owns: context creation, response validation, stats tracking, error wrapping, stream lifecycle
- Change: move validate+sequencer+writer orchestration behind one native function per protocol; TS keeps only `convertResponseToJsonToSse(...)` -> Readable IO shell
### P1: responses-sse-to-json-converter.ts - 435 lines
- TS owns stream chunking, 3-phase timeout (noFrame/preAnchor/contentIdle), event accumulation, function_call arg buffer
- Rust has batch materialization but no streaming incremental state machine
- Change: Rust streaming decode state machine that accepts chunks one by one and emits final JSON on terminal signal
### P2: writer.ts - 359 lines
- IO / backpressure / heartbeat, protocol dispatch still TS
- Change: either move to transport-only or leave as is since it's not semantic

## pre-existing gap
- `execute-pipeline-failover.spec.ts` 6 tests fail because mock `processIncoming` returns typed HTTP response object; executor conversion path creates Error losing retry plan check. Covered by other failover tests.

## Verification
- sse-rust-parity 6 suites 33/33 PASS
- verify:sse-architecture-boundary PASS
- verify:function-map-compile-gate PASS
- npx tsc --noEmit PASS

# 2026-07-03: provider probe feature removed before commit

- Decision: active provider runtime probe was removed from `routecodex restart` and `routecodex port status`; startup/restart must not actively test providers because transient upstream/key failures can block or mislead boot diagnostics.
- Current behavior: VR diagnostics remain read-only Rust status/dry-run projection. `routecodex port status 5520 --json` calls only `/_routecodex/diagnostics/virtual-router/status`; `?probe=1` is ignored by the HTTP status route and never returns `providerProbe`.
- Guard: `port-command.spec.ts` rejects removed `--probe`; `virtual-router-diagnostics.spec.ts` asserts `status?probe=1` returns normal VR status without `providerProbe`.
- Evidence: residue scan for `providerProbe|probeAvailableProviders|provider_probe|status?probe|--probe|Probing provider|checkHealth?` in HTTP/CLI focused paths returns 0; focused Jest `virtual-router-diagnostics + port-command + restart-command` PASS 19/19; root `tsc --noEmit` PASS; `verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS; `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` PASS; `verify:vr-no-ts-runtime` PASS; `git diff --check` PASS; global install/restart live version `0.90.3525`, `/health` ready, status JSON `providerProbePresent=false`.

# 2026-07-03: HubPipeline materialized request control plan moved to native helper

- Slice: `hub-pipeline.ts` no longer owns `endpoint/entryEndpoint/providerProtocol/processMode/direction/stage/stream/hubEntryMode/policyOverride/shadowCompare/disableSnapshots` materialization rules. TS now calls `buildHubPipelineMaterializedRequestPlanWithNative()` and only applies the native plan to build `NormalizedRequest`.
- Rust/NAPI: added `build_hub_pipeline_materialized_request_plan` plus JSON export `buildHubPipelineMaterializedRequestPlanJson`; Rust owns HubPipeline ingress control-field normalization and cleanup. TS keeps payload IO, MetadataCenter symbol preservation, and native-plan application.
- Gate: `hub-pipeline-stage-residue-audit.spec.ts` now requires the native materialized-request plan helper and forbids TS ownership of `__hubEntry`, `__hubPolicyOverride`, `__hubShadowCompare`, `__disableHubSnapshots`, direction/stage normalization, and `processMode` materialization.
- Evidence: `cargo test -p router-hotpath-napi hub_pipeline_materialized --lib -- --nocapture` PASS 2/2; `npx tsc -p sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false` PASS; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` PASS; `npm run build:native-hotpath` PASS; `npm run verify:servertool-rust-only` PASS; `npm run verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS; `npm run verify:architecture-mainline-call-map` PASS; `npm run verify:architecture-review-surface-light` PASS; `git diff --check` PASS; `tests/sharedmodule/hub-pipeline-stage-residue-audit.spec.ts` PASS 152/152.
- Remaining gap: aggregate closeout still needs live/replay validation and the remaining VR/Responses slices from the phase plan.

# 2026-07-03: req_outbound native adapter context field extraction moved to Rust

- Slice: `native-adapter-context.ts` no longer reads `runtimeControl/requestTruth/providerObservation/target` or trims/fail-fast checks fields in TS. TS only reads the bound MetadataCenter snapshot carrier and calls `buildNativeReqOutboundCompatAdapterContextWithNative()`.
- Rust owner: `req_outbound_stage3_compat` now exports `buildNativeReqOutboundCompatAdapterContextJson`; Rust extracts `providerProtocol`, provider target/key/model, request truth, route/session/conversation fields from `metadataCenterSnapshot` and fails fast when `runtimeControl.providerProtocol` is missing.
- Gate: `verify-responses-request-compat-rust-only.mjs` now requires the native adapter-context builder/export and forbids TS-side `metadataCenterSnapshot?.runtimeControl/requestTruth/providerObservation`, `runtimeControl?.providerProtocol`, and local `readStringFrom` markers.

- Current unrelated blocker: `npx tsc -p sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false` fails on pre-existing/unrelated dirty SSE writer removal/imports (`src/sse/json-to-sse/* -> ../shared/writer.js`), not this adapter-context slice.

# 2026-07-03: request-stage HubPipeline result projection moved to Rust

- Slice: `hub-pipeline-execute-request-stage.ts` no longer hand-builds `HubPipelineResult` from `providerPayload`, `metadata.target`, `metadata.routingDecision`, `metadata.routingDiagnostics`, diagnostics, or entry-mode standardizedRequest. TS now calls `buildRequestStageHubPipelineResultWithNative()` after native result planning and only performs error throw, MetadataCenter write IO, and hub-stage timing IO.

# 2026-07-03: snapshot recorder write options moved to Rust

TS now normalizes payload, reads the bound MetadataCenter snapshot carrier, calls `buildSnapshotRecorderWriteOptionsWithNative()`, and keeps only non-blocking snapshot write IO.

# 2026-07-04: 5555 Responses SSE request_id + cooldown restart recovery

- Issue 1: installed 0.90.3538 `/v1/responses` failed captured MiniMax Anthropic SSE with `Responses SSE event sequence missing request_id`.
- Root cause: installed Rust SSE runtime dispatch did not accept TS wrapper snake_case `request_id` / `body_text` under `rename_all = "camelCase"`. Current source already contains aliases; added focused Rust coverage and installed `routecodex/rcc@0.90.3541`.
- Evidence: installed 0.90.3541 replay of `~/.rcc/codex-samples/openai-responses/ports/5555/req_1783095044093_2201763d/provider-response_3.json` produced SSE with `response.created`, `response.done`, and original request_id.
- Issue 2: `~/.rcc/sessions/127.0.0.1_5555/provider-health.json` retained expired `orangeai.1.glm-5.2` cooldown after restart.
- Root cause: expired persisted cooldown was skipped on import but not persisted back immediately, so disk could still show stale tripped state until another health write.
- Guard: `refresh_provider_health_from_store_persists_pruned_expired_cooldown` proves engine refresh writes cleaned provider-health after pruning expired cooldown.
- Live evidence: 5555 VR status reports `orangeai.1.glm-5.2` `state=healthy`, `cooldownExpiresAt=null`, `available=true`; live 5555 SSE smoke returned `response.created`, `response.completed`, `response.done` with request_id on `0.90.3541`.
- Runtime note: 5555 is a localPort under serverId 5520. Both `routecodex` and `rcc` must be installed in sync; live process was `rcc`, not only `routecodex`.

# 2026-07-04: rcc lifecycle stop/start port-group closure

- Symptom: `rcc stop` only handled 5520 and said no server, while `rcc start --snap` resolved `4444, 5520, 5555, 10000` then exited on `4444 already_running_unmanaged`.
- Root causes:
  - `resolvePortGroupFromConfig({ targetPort })` collapsed a matched multi-port config to `[targetPort]`, so stop/restart never expanded the full group.
  - `stop` trusted PID discovery only; when PID cache/listener discovery returned empty, it skipped `/shutdown` even though `/health` was still live.
  - `start` defaulted to non-restart mode, so healthy no-PID RouteCodex listeners caused `already_running_unmanaged`.
  - guardian finalize/report errors were treated as stop failure after the actual server ports were already stopped.
- Fix:
  - Added `includeSiblingsForTarget` to port-group resolver and used it from stop/restart/start release group resolution.
  - `stop` now attempts HTTP `/shutdown` even when no PID is found, waits for health to disappear, and treats guardian finalize/report failures as non-blocking lifecycle telemetry.
  - `start` now owns the managed runtime slot by default; `--no-restart` keeps the old check-only behavior.
- Evidence:
  - Focused Jest `tests/cli/start-command.spec.ts`, `tests/cli/stop-command.spec.ts`, `tests/cli/port-utils.spec.ts` PASS 31/31.
  - `build:min` PASS; `pack:rcc` PASS; global install of both `routecodex-0.90.3542.tgz` and `rcc-0.90.3542.tgz` PASS.
  - Live `rcc stop` on down group now resolves all four ports and exits 0 despite guardian finalize rejection.
  - Live `rcc start --snap` brought up 4444/5520/5555/10000 on global 0.90.3542. With existing service running, `ROUTECODEX_START_DAEMON=1 rcc start --snap` sent shutdown for the old group, exited 0, and all four ports returned `/health` 0.90.3542.

# 2026-07-04 lifecycle correction final evidence

- Root cause confirmed in installed old package: `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/{routecodex,rcc}/dist/cli/commands/start.js` had `options.restart !== false`, so plain `start` was restart-like by default.
- Source fix: `src/cli/commands/start.ts` uses `options.restart === true || options.exclusive === true`; focused CLI tests lock `[false, true]` for default start vs explicit `--restart`.
- Gate fixes: `verify:servertool-rust-only` now checks deleted chat-process servertool wrapper semantics through `native-servertool-core-semantics.ts`; sharedmodule source self-reference to `rcc-llmswitch-core/native/servertool-wrapper` compiles through `sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/types/servertool-wrapper.d.ts`, avoiding TS5055 from `dist/*.d.ts` inputs.
- Verification: `npm run jest:run -- --runInBand --runTestsByPath tests/cli/start-command.spec.ts tests/cli/stop-command.spec.ts tests/cli/port-utils.spec.ts` PASS 32/32; `npx tsc -p sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false` PASS; `node scripts/build-core.mjs` PASS; `npm run build:min` PASS; `npm run pack:rcc` PASS.
- Install: `npm install -g artifacts/pack/routecodex-0.90.3546.tgz artifacts/pack/rcc-0.90.3546.tgz` PASS; `routecodex --version` and `rcc --version` both 0.90.3546; installed dist contains `options.restart === true`.
- Live non-disruptive smoke: `routecodex start --port 5555` returned `already_running_unmanaged`; 5520/5555 `/health` stayed OK. No intentional restart was run, so live server process still reports 0.90.3542 until Jason chooses to restart.

# 2026-07-04 correction: start default restart was wrong

- User-visible symptom: foreground server printed SIGTERM / graceful shutdown after `start`-style recovery work, making `restart`/`start` look like it only stopped the service.
- Corrected root cause: dirty `start.ts` changed plain `start` to `restart` by default (`options.restart !== false`), and one stop-command test had no `fetchImpl` mock, so it could hit live `/shutdown`.
- Fix: restored plain start to non-disruptive (`options.restart === true || options.exclusive === true`), kept explicit `--restart`, and mocked no-server stop tests so unit tests cannot touch live RouteCodex.
- Evidence: focused CLI Jest PASS 32/32; sharedmodule tsc PASS; 5520/5555 `/health` OK on 0.90.3542; 5555 `/v1/responses` SSE smoke `routecodex-start-no-auto-restart-20260704T110818` returned `response.created`, `response.completed`, `response.done` with request_id.

# 2026-07-04: model capacity text normalized to retryable 429

- Symptom: upstream/provider error text `Selected model is at capacity. Please try a different model.` carried no HTTP status/code in the visible failure, so RouteCodex did not preserve a canonical 429 identity in the provider error catalog.
- Owner: `error.provider_failure_policy`; allowed source owner is `src/providers/core/runtime/provider-error-catalog.ts`, with policy consumption in `provider-failure-policy-impl.ts` and HTTP error projection in `provider-http-executor-utils.ts`.
- Fix: `normalizeKnownProviderError()` now maps `selected model is at capacity` / `model is at capacity` + `try a different model` to existing recoverable `HTTP_429` (`429.1000`), not to `INSUFFICIENT_QUOTA`.
- Tests added: catalog status/key/class regression, provider failure action plan regression, HTTP error projection regression.
- Evidence: red first run showed catalog returned `undefined`; then `npm run jest:run -- --runInBand --runTestsByPath tests/providers/core/runtime/provider-error-catalog.spec.ts tests/providers/core/runtime/provider-failure-policy.spec.ts tests/providers/core/runtime/provider-http-executor-utils.spec.ts` PASS 45/45; manual source replay showed catalog `HTTP_429`, normalized `statusCode/status=429`, policy `shouldRetry=true`, `action=reroute_explicit_alternative`, `decisionLabel=exclude_and_reroute`; `verify:function-map-compile-gate` PASS; scoped `git diff --check` PASS.
- Remaining blockers: `npm run build:base` currently fails on unrelated dirty llmswitch-core/servertool TS errors; `verify:provider-failure-ban-blackbox` still fails on existing native hotpath `resolveHubPipelineRequestProviderProtocolJson` unavailable/non-string result. No global install/live restart claimed for this fix.

# 2026-07-03: `/v1/responses` image request stack overflow fixed in direct runtime metadata projection

- Symptom: image-bearing `/v1/responses` on 5520 failed with `[llmswitch-bridge] evaluateResponsesDirectRouteDecisionJson JSON stringify failed: Maximum call stack size exceeded`; removing the image made the request pass the failing point.
- Root cause: direct path was passing live metadata/control objects into JSON bridge/provider runtime metadata. Image requests made the metadata graph include large request input plus cyclic `metadataCenterSnapshot`/live carrier references, so JSON stringify recursed.
- Fix: `src/server/runtime/http-server/index.ts` now passes direct provider calls through `buildDirectProviderRuntimeMetadata()`, which projects only primitive provider runtime controls (`entryEndpoint`, port fields, routing group, client request id, stream timeout primitives, and `__responsesDirectPassthrough`). It does not carry request `input`, full `metadataCenterSnapshot`, `metadataForHub`, or cyclic live objects.
- Regression: `tests/server/runtime/http-server/direct-runtime-metadata.spec.ts` locks cyclic image metadata as JSON-serializable and verifies forbidden live fields are not projected. Existing direct passthrough contract tests still pass.
- Evidence: focused Jest `direct-runtime-metadata + direct-passthrough-payload` PASS 6/6; `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` PASS; direct shape gates PASS (`contract`, `rust-first`, `native-availability`, `no-ts-fallback`); global install version `0.90.3532`; `routecodex restart --port 5520` previously PASS; `/health` ready on 5520.
- Live replay: image smoke request `openai-responses-router-gpt-5.4-20260703T154838519-455135-1532` reached VR multimodal and provider switching. No new `Maximum call stack` or `evaluateResponsesDirectRouteDecisionJson` error appeared for that request. It then exposed real upstream errors: ASXS `HTTP_400` invalid image data, ASXS backup `HTTP_401`, XL `HTTP_403` insufficient quota, then client timeout/disconnect.
- Remaining unrelated blocker: `npm run build:base` currently fails before build on dirty SSE architecture map state: missing `sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/src/sse/json-to-sse/sequencers/anthropic-sequencer.ts` and `gemini-sequencer.ts` caller files in `verify:architecture-mainline-call-map`.

# 2026-07-03: Codex tool-call transcript leak fixed at Responses client projection

- Symptom: Codex UI displayed model text such as `Assistant requested tool calls: - id=call_00... type=function name=exec_command arguments=...`; session and `~/.rcc/codex-samples/openai-responses/ports/5555/.../client-response.json` proved RouteCodex emitted it as client-visible `/v1/responses` `output_text`, not merely as structured tool call rendering.
- Root cause: the first governance/display sanitizer green path was not the live `/v1/responses` exit. The actual live owner is `hub.response_responses_client_projection`; SSE and client response projection were forwarding provider text chunks into `response.output_text.delta/done` and final `output_text`.
- Fix: `hub_resp_outbound_client_semantics_blocks/client_tool_args.rs` now sanitizes only client-visible display text fields (`output_text`, output_text content `text`, `response.output_text.delta`, `response.output_text.done`) with `strip_tool_markup_for_display_text`, preserving structured `function_call.arguments`.
- Guard: added Rust regressions for both full Responses client payload projection and SSE frame projection, plus the existing governance sanitizer regression for the Codex transcript marker.
- Release evidence: focused Rust leak tests PASS 3/3; `verify:hub-response-responses-chat-projection` PASS; native hotpath build PASS; sharedmodule `tsc` PASS; function-map gate PASS; `build:min` PASS; `pack:rcc` produced synchronized `routecodex-0.90.3536.tgz` and `rcc-0.90.3536.tgz`; both globally installed as release buildTime `2026-07-03T12:58:21.144Z`.
- Live evidence: installed 5555 `/health` ready on `0.90.3536`; live `/v1/responses` replay marker `routecodex-tool-leak-live-20260703T210111` returned `response.completed`; leak scan for `Assistant requested tool calls|call_00_wpSa|virtual_router_hit_log` returned `LEAK_PRESENT=0`.

# 2026-07-03: P0-1 servertool run-engine entry/prepass application moved to Rust

- Slice: `run-server-side-tool-engine-shell.ts` no longer branches directly on `entryPreflightDecision.action === 'return_result'` or `enginePrepassDecision.action === 'return_result'`. TS now calls Rust-owned application planners and only applies `returnResult/baseObject/result` fields.
- Rust owner: `servertool-core/src/server_side_tool_entry_contract.rs` owns `plan_servertool_run_engine_entry_preflight_application`; `servertool-core/src/engine_prepass_action_contract.rs` owns `plan_servertool_run_engine_prepass_application`. `router-hotpath-napi` exports both JSON bridge functions.
